mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad demand contraction that depresses corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series benchmark or yields positive 60d returns in >55% of co-declining periods over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index drop below its 12-month moving average during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances during tightening periods or the moving average crossover shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation growth combined with a rising federal funds rate creates stagflationary pressure that forces equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.5% and FEDFUNDS increases
failure_condition: The interaction term produces no incremental predictive power over individual series or yields positive 20d returns in >65% of triggered cases over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands consumer spending capacity and supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of positive payroll growth periods or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that accelerate portfolio deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of contraction quarters or the threshold signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase indicates resolving supply constraints that boost equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered instances during recovery regimes or the signal shows no predictive edge (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 4-year walk-forward period.
